summary: Completed frontend-only testing for the new /download page + Landing hero redirect + /app Studio regression. ALL 6 target flows passed cleanly on the preview URL. OS detection correctly identified Linux (headless Chromium), the detected panel is ordered first in the DOM, and all 8 installer links (3 Windows + 2 macOS + 3 Linux) point at github.com/.../releases/latest/download/* as expected. Footer links (all-releases / use-browser / back) wired correctly. Landing hero CTA reads 'Download' and routes to /download (not /app). /app Studio regression also passes — canvas-bg present and 6 seed nodes visibly render.

tests_executed.exactly_one_detected: PASS — data-detected='true' only on 'linux' (headless CI is Linux, as expected)
tests_executed.link_hrefs_github_releases: PASS — all 8 links contain 'github.com' AND 'releases/latest/download'. Repo path is placeholder 'YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/mind-mapper' as documented by main agent; user will set REACT_APP_DESKTOP_GITHUB_REPO later.
tests_executed.hero_click_navigates_to_download: PASS — after click URL is /download (not /app) and download-page visible
tests_executed.studio_app_regression: PASS — /app?unlock=mind-mapper67 loads, [data-role='canvas-bg'] present, and screenshot confirms 6 seed nodes visible (Core Concept + Idea One–Five). Generic node selector in test returned 0 (the Studio uses SVG/custom markup with no data-testid on node wrappers), but the canvas and nodes clearly render — this is a test-selector gap, not a functional regression.

action_items: (Optional / low priority) Add a stable data-testid on Studio node wrappers (e.g., data-testid='mindmap-node') so future regression tests can assert node count without fragile SVG selectors. Current test had to fall back on a screenshot for verification.

(Reminder for deploy time, not a bug) Set REACT_APP_DESKTOP_GITHUB_REPO in frontend/.env once the GitHub repo is live — currently all 8 installer hrefs point at github.com/YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/mind-mapper/releases/latest/download/*.

(Nice-to-have) detectMacArch() currently always returns 'arm64' and is invoked only via `void detectMacArch;` inside useEffect — i.e., it's dead code today. Either wire it into the mac panel (prefer the Apple-Silicon link when detected) or drop it to avoid future confusion.
critical_code_review_comments: Download.jsx is self-contained, env-driven (REACT_APP_DESKTOP_GITHUB_REPO), and under 250 lines — clean.
OS detection heuristic is pragmatic: regex on userAgent+platform, falls back to 'unknown' → primary defaults to 'windows'. Matches the request's expected behavior.
The 401s seen in console come from existing /api/auth/me background call on unauth'd pages — unrelated to this change, pre-existing across iterations.
test_credentials: App gate unlock param: ?unlock=mind-mapper67 (from request, no /app/memory/test_credentials.md changes needed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The /download page, Electron scaffolding (/app/electron/), and CI workflow (/app/.github/workflows/electron-build.yml) are in place. Electron itself is scaffolding-only — no GH release exists yet, so installer download links 404 today by design. Don't retest the hrefs' resolution; test only structure + wiring as done here. Landing hero now routes to /download (not /app); the *secondary* 'Open the Studio' CTA at the bottom of the landing (data-testid='cta-launch-bottom') still points at /app — which is correct.
